justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) /health stub identified as 'a lie — reports healthy when dependencies are down, always returns 200'; (2) consequence named — 'load balancers and orchestrators will route traffic to instances that cannot actually process work'; (3) specific dependency checks recommended — DB connectivity (SELECT 1), queue accessibility, SendGrid API reachability with cache TTL; (4) circuit breaker absence on SendGrid flagged as 'single highest-priority gap' and escalated; (5) verdict is NOT READY; (6) logging coverage marked 'adequate' and metrics coverage acknowledged as partial with specific gaps identified.

observations.text: Health check stub called out explicitly as an escalation: 'Health endpoint is a lie — reports healthy when dependencies are down'
Circuit breaker absence flagged as the highest-priority gap with detailed cascading failure analysis (10s timeout × 3 retries × concurrent requests = pool exhaustion)
Specific health check recommendations go beyond minimum: SELECT 1 for DB, queue accessibility, SendGrid reachability with cache TTL
Verdict is NOT READY with deployment_ready: false

Goes beyond rubric requirements with additional valuable findings: idempotency key gap, dead-letter queue need, poison message handling, and detailed failure mode analysis for partial degradation scenarios
Logging correctly noted as adequate coverage while identifying specific structured logging gaps for improvement
